title: Story Garden Adventure
focus: Listening, speaking, story sequence, emotions and creative expression
summary: Children grow a magical story garden by listening to short stories, arranging picture events and expressing feelings.
outcomes: Retell a simple story using beginning, middle and ending.
Recognise basic emotions shown by characters.
Use words, drawings or movement to respond creatively.
assessment: Oral retelling, picture sequencing and teacher observation.

title: Shape and Motion Playground
focus: Shapes, positions, movement, balance and spatial vocabulary
summary: Learners design a virtual playground using shapes and test how objects move, roll and balance.
outcomes: Identify common two-dimensional and three-dimensional shapes.
Use position words such as above, below, beside and between.
Describe simple movement such as rolling, sliding and spinning.
assessment: Shape identification and guided movement challenge.

title: Phonics Rescue Station
focus: Letter-sound relationships, blending, vocabulary and simple sentence reading
summary: Students rescue lost word robots by matching sounds, blending letters and reading short sentences.
outcomes: Match selected letters and common sounds.
Blend sounds to read simple words.
Understand short instructions and sentences.
assessment: Sound matching, word reading and sentence comprehension.

title: Mini Makers Workshop
focus: Materials, simple construction, measurement and safe tool habits
summary: Young makers choose materials and build simple objects such as bridges, containers and toy vehicles.
outcomes: Recognise common materials and their basic properties.
Measure and compare length using appropriate units or non-standard units.
Follow a simple design-and-build sequence safely.
product: A digital model with a short explanation.

title: Community Helpers Mission
focus: Community roles, communication, responsibility and emergency awareness
summary: Students solve community problems by selecting the correct helper and communicating clear requests.
outcomes: Identify common community helpers and their roles.
Use polite and clear language to ask for help.
Recognise appropriate responses to simple emergencies.

title: Pattern and Coding Trail
focus: Patterns, sequences, direction, algorithms and debugging
summary: Guide a friendly robot along a jungle trail by recognising patterns and arranging movement instructions.
outcomes: Continue visual and number patterns.
Arrange step-by-step instructions in the correct order.
Identify and correct a simple error in a sequence.

title: Sabah River Explorer
focus: Freshwater habitats, observation, conservation and descriptive reporting
summary: Students explore a fictional Sabah river, identify organisms and investigate how human actions affect water quality.
outcomes: Identify selected living and non-living river components.
Record simple field observations in a table.
Suggest actions that protect freshwater environments.
assessment: Observation table and environmental decision task.

title: Storybook Creator Studio
focus: Narrative structure, vocabulary, dialogue, illustration and editing
summary: Learners create a short illustrated story using a beginning, problem, solution and ending.
outcomes: Organise a story into a logical sequence.
Use descriptive vocabulary and simple dialogue.
Review writing for basic spelling and punctuation.

title: Safe Internet Junior
focus: Passwords, privacy, online communication, scams and responsible behaviour
summary: Students protect a virtual school network by making safe online decisions.
outcomes: Recognise characteristics of a stronger password.
Identify personal information that should not be shared publicly.
Respond appropriately to suspicious messages and cyberbullying.

title: Plant Growth Research Station
focus: Plant needs, variables, measurement, data tables and scientific reporting
summary: Students conduct a simulated plant-growth investigation by controlling light, water and soil conditions.
outcomes: Identify variables in a simple investigation.
Measure and record plant growth over time.
Draw a conclusion using collected evidence.

title: Young Inventor Design Lab
focus: Problem identification, sketches, mechanisms, testing and improvement
summary: Students invent a simple product to solve an everyday school or home problem.
outcomes: Define a practical user problem.
Produce and label a design sketch.
Evaluate a prototype and recommend improvements.
assessment: Design brief, labelled sketch and evaluation.

title: Local Economy Market Challenge
focus: Local products, supply and demand, pricing, profit and responsible consumption
summary: Students manage a virtual local market stall while balancing stock, price, customer demand and waste.
outcomes: Calculate revenue, cost and simple profit.
Explain how supply and demand may influence price.
Make choices that reduce waste and support local producers.

title: Civic Decision Lab
focus: Representation, respectful discussion, voting, data and community decisions
summary: Students debate school-community proposals and use fair voting processes to reach decisions.
outcomes: Differentiate facts, opinions and reasons.
Participate in respectful discussion and decision-making.
Interpret vote totals and percentages.

title: Earth and Space Observatory
focus: Solar system, rotation, revolution, scale and scientific models
summary: Students operate a virtual observatory and investigate day, night, seasons and planetary relationships.
outcomes: Describe rotation and revolution using a model.
Identify selected objects in the Solar System.
Interpret simplified scale and orbital data.

title: Digital Storytelling Studio
focus: Script writing, media language, storyboarding, copyright and presentation
summary: Students plan a short educational video using responsible media and storytelling techniques.
outcomes: Develop a clear audience, purpose and message.
Create a storyboard using shots, narration and dialogue.
Apply basic copyright and attribution practices.

title: Food Science Investigation
focus: Nutrients, food tests, experimental variables, data and balanced nutrition
summary: Students test fictional food samples and use evidence to design a balanced meal.
outcomes: Describe functions of major nutrient groups.
Interpret outcomes of selected food-test simulations.
Evaluate a meal using nutritional evidence.

title: Renewable Island Operations
focus: Renewable resources, energy demand, storage, weather and system reliability
summary: Students manage an island energy system using solar, wind, hydro and battery storage.
outcomes: Compare selected renewable-energy sources.
Interpret energy demand and generation data.
Design a more reliable low-carbon energy mix.

title: Language Debate Arena
focus: Claims, evidence, rebuttal, persuasive language and respectful communication
summary: Students prepare and deliver structured arguments on age-appropriate school and community issues.
outcomes: Construct a claim supported by relevant evidence.
Differentiate strong reasoning from unsupported assertion.
Respond respectfully to an opposing argument.

title: Cyber Safety Operations Centre
focus: Phishing, malware, authentication, backups, incident response and digital ethics
summary: Students respond to simulated cyber incidents affecting a fictional school network.
outcomes: Recognise common indicators of phishing and malicious activity.
Explain the purpose of authentication, updates and backups.
Prioritise safe and ethical incident-response actions.
assessment: Cyber incident scenarios and response plan.

title: Sustainable Agriculture Simulator
focus: Soil, water, crop planning, productivity, biodiversity and sustainable farming
summary: Students manage a virtual farm while balancing crop yield, soil quality, water and environmental impact.
outcomes: Relate soil, water and climate factors to crop growth.
Interpret yield and resource-use data.
Recommend farming practices that improve sustainability.

title: Entrepreneurship and E-Commerce Lab
focus: Customer needs, digital storefronts, pricing, marketing metrics and consumer protection
summary: Students launch a fictional online product and manage costs, pricing, promotion and customer service.
outcomes: Develop a customer profile and value proposition.
Calculate cost, revenue, profit and conversion rate.
Apply responsible advertising and consumer-protection principles.

title: Chemistry in Everyday Life Lab
focus: Acids and alkalis, reaction evidence, household chemicals, concentration and safety
summary: Students analyse fictional household substances and make evidence-based safety decisions.
outcomes: Classify selected substances using simple chemical evidence.
Recognise indicators of chemical change.
Apply safe handling and labelling practices.

title: AI Ethics and Society Forum
focus: AI systems, bias, privacy, transparency, accountability and evidence-based discussion
summary: Students examine fictional AI use cases in schools, workplaces and public services.
outcomes: Explain at a basic level how data influences AI outputs.
Identify risks involving bias, privacy and over-reliance.
Develop a balanced recommendation for responsible AI use.

title: Climate Adaptation City Planner
focus: Climate risk, flooding, heat, infrastructure, data and adaptation planning
summary: Students redesign a fictional city to reduce flood and heat risks under budget constraints.
outcomes: Interpret simplified climate and hazard data.
Compare structural and nature-based adaptation options.
Prioritise interventions using risk, cost and social impact.
assessment: City plan justification and trade-off analysis.
